title: quercus radiata
intro: Quercus radiata, also known as the golden oak or canyon live oak, is a species of evergreen oak tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The bark and leaves of the Quercus radiata have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant's medicinal properties also make it beneficial for improving cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of certain cancers, and supporting immune function. Quercus radiata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal remedies.
